repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Velar SVAutobiography P550 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Worth it on a £30k+ Velar SVAutobiography
Viable on a rare, high-value vehicle

buyingChecks.title: Buying This SVAutobiography P550? Check These First
buyIf: * Full JLR service history with documented cooling system inspections
* No cold-start rattling (supercharger coupler or timing chain health)
* No coolant loss or white residue around coolant pipes
* Supercharger coupler replaced as preventative at 50k+ miles
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattling — budget for supercharger coupler or timing chain work
* Unexplained coolant loss — cooling system issues may be developing
* Overheating history — the AJ133 doesn't forgive cooling system neglect
* No service history — the V8 is unforgiving of neglect
* No specialist V8 diagnostic capability locally — you'll need one

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Velar SVAutobiography P550 is a rare and special vehicle — a 5.0-litre supercharged V8 in a Velar body. The post-2013 AJ133 is a robust engine with no major design flaws, but it has known weak points: supercharger coupler wear, cooling system leaks, and timing chain tensioner wear (though much improved on post-2013 units). With typical Velar SVAutobiography values of £30,000–£50,000, a £8,000–£12,000 engine replacement is borderline but often worth it for a cherished example. Due diligence on the cooling system and supercharger is absolutely essential before purchase.

cta.label: → Compare SVAutobiography P550 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Velar SVAutobiography P550 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start Rattle — Listen carefully for 1–2 second rattle (supercharger coupler) or extended rattle (timing chain)
* Service History — Including coolant system inspections and supercharger service — see the map above
* Coolant Level — Check reservoir when cold; any low or loss is a red flag
* Coolant Pipes — Inspect for white residue around crossover pipes and water pump area
* Supercharger Health — Listen for unusual noises; check for any performance hesitation
* Oil Condition — Check for any metallic particles or discoloration
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes read; unresolved issues may indicate timing or cooling problems
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (V8 is generally clean but NOx sensors can fail)

items.question: How much does a Range Rover Velar SVAutobiography P550 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned SVAutobiography P550 engine typically costs £8,000–£12,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500 depending on the specialist. Used engines can be found from £3,000–£5,500, but condition varies dramatically and specialist inspection is essential. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Velar.

items.question: Is the Range Rover Velar SVAutobiography P550 engine reliable?
items.answer: The post-2013 AJ133 is a significantly improved engine over the early units — the timing chain tensioner issue was resolved. It's a robust, high-performance V8 with known weak points: supercharger coupler wear, cooling system leaks (water pump and crossover pipes), and some timing chain tensioner wear at higher mileages. With proper maintenance and attention to the cooling system, it's a reliable and rewarding engine.

items.question: What are the common problems with the Range Rover Velar SVAutobiography P550?
items.answer: The main issues are supercharger coupler wear (causing a rattle on cold start), cooling system leaks from the water pump and coolant crossover pipes, and timing chain tensioner wear (though much improved on post-2013 units). None are catastrophic if caught early — regular maintenance and monitoring are essential.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Velar SVAutobiography P550 engine, or should I scrap the vehicle?
items.answer: Given the rarity and value of the Velar SVAutobiography P550 (typical £30,000–£50,000 for a good example), a £8,000–£12,000 engine replacement is borderline but often worth it for a cherished example. These are rare halo cars — values have held well. However, factor in the vehicle's overall condition and your emotional connection to the car.

items.question: How do I find my Range Rover Velar SVAutobiography P550 engine code?
items.answer: The engine code is AJ133, found on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation. It's the 5.0-litre supercharged V8. If you're unsure, enter your registration on our site and we'll confirm it for free.

items.question: Can I repair a supercharger coupler, or does the supercharger need replacement?
items.answer: The coupler can be replaced — it's a wear item that dampens the supercharger's engagement. A replacement coupler costs £500–£1,200. If the coupler fails completely, the supercharger itself may need replacement (£3,000–£5,000). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the coupler inspected.

faq.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists.
